description: Caching tenant-scoped data under a key that omits the tenant serves one tenant's cached values to another.
body: Failure mode: a cache key is built from a resource id or query without the tenant, so the first tenant to populate it determines what every tenant sees. Detection signals: tenant B receives tenant A's data on a cache hit; the cache key contains no tenant component; the leak appears only under cache hits. Do not cache tenant data with a tenant-blind key. Safer fallback: include the tenant id in every cache key, scope invalidation per tenant, verify with a test that values never cross tenants, and treat any occurrence as a leak requiring escalation.
